name: Lime-Peanut-Ginger Chicken
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons cornstarch diluted with 2 T water
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut chicken into bite-size pieces and marinate in lime juice for at least 2 hours in the refrigerator.
2. Make a dressing by chopping garlic, ginger, and peppercorns in a food processor or blender until pepper is no longer whole (it will probably be impossible to break up all peppercorns).
3. Blend basil, soy sauce, vinegar, honey, water, and grated lemon peel into the dressing. Pour 2 tablespoons of olive oil into the processor or blender and process until smooth. Set dressing aside.
4. Remove chicken from marinade and sauté it in sesame oil and the remaining 3 tablespoons of olive oil until it turns white.
5. Add dressing and stir until chicken is well coated and sauce is warm.
6. Mix in peanut butter. Stir in mushrooms, add cornstarch mixture to thicken sauce.
7. Sprinkle green onions on top and serve over rice.
